arabic: تُعَدُّ النِّيَّةُ الصَّالِحَةُ رُكْنًا أَسَاسِيًّا فِي قَبُولِ الْأَعْمَالِ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى، فَالْمُؤْمِنُ يَسْعَى دَائِمًا لِتَصْحِيحِ مَقْصِدِهِ لِيَنَالَ الْأَجْرَ وَالثَّوَابَ. وَقَدْ جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الشَّرِيفِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ: «إِنَّمَا الْأَعْمَالُ بِالنِّيَّاتِ، وَإِنَّمَا لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مَا نَوَى» [رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ فِي كِتَابِ بَدْءِ الْوَحْيِ]. يُؤَكِّدُ الْإِمَامُ ابْنُ الْقَيِّمِ أَنَّ الْإِخْلَاصَ هُوَ تَوْحِيدُ الْمَطْلَبِ، بِحَيْثُ لَا يَبْتَغِي الْعَبْدُ بِعَمَلِهِ إِلَّا وَجْهَ اللَّهِ. لِذَلِكَ، يَجِبُ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِ أَنْ يُرَاقِبَ قَلْبَهُ قَبْلَ الْبَدْءِ فِي أَيِّ عِبَادَةٍ أَوْ فِعْلٍ خَيْرِيٍّ. إِنَّ تَجْرِيدَ الْإِخْلَاصِ لِلَّهِ يُحَوِّلُ الْعَادَاتِ الْيَوْمِيَّةَ إِلَى عِبَادَاتٍ عَظِيمَةٍ تَرْفَعُ دَرَجَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنِ فِي الْآخِرَةِ. فَالْمَقْصِدُ الْحَسَنُ هُوَ رُوحُ الْعَمَلِ وَبِدُونِهِ يَصِيرُ الْفِعْلُ جَسَدًا بِلَا رُوحٍ.
translation: Iskrena namjera smatra se osnovnim stubom za primanje djela kod Allaha Uzvišenog, te vjernik uvijek nastoji ispraviti svoj cilj kako bi stekao nagradu i sevap. U plemenitom hadisu od Omera ibn el-Hattaba, radijallahu anhu, stoji: 'Djela se vrednuju prema namjerama i svakom čovjeku pripada ono što je nanijetio' [Bilježi Buhari u Knjizi o početku objave]. Imam Ibn el-Kayyim naglašava da je ihlas (iskrenost) jedinstvo cilja, tako da rob svojim djelom ne traži ništa osim Allahovog lica. Zbog toga, musliman mora preispitati svoje srce prije početka bilo kojeg ibadeta ili dobrog djela. Potpuna iskrenost prema Allahu pretvara svakodnevne navike u velike ibadete koji podižu stepene vjernika na ahiretu. Dobra namjera je duša djela, a bez nje djelo postaje tijelo bez duše.
translationEn: Sincere intention is considered a fundamental pillar for the acceptance of deeds by Allah the Almighty, and the believer always strives to correct his purpose to attain reward. It is stated in the noble hadith from Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him): 'Actions are but by intentions, and every person shall have only that which he intended' [Narrated by al-Bukhari in the Book of the Beginning of Revelation]. Imam Ibn al-Qayyim emphasizes that sincerity (Ikhlas) is the unification of the objective, such that the servant seeks nothing with his deed except the face of Allah. Therefore, a Muslim must monitor his heart before starting any worship or charitable act. Pure sincerity for Allah transforms daily habits into great acts of worship that raise the believer's ranks in the Hereafter. A good intention is the soul of the deed, and without it, the action becomes a body without a soul.
